steps.title: Identify the decay factor
steps.content: When mass decreases by 27 percent, 27 percent is lost, leaving 73 percent. The decay factor (the multiplier each period) is \$0.73$, not \$0.27$.

steps.title: Set up the exponential decay model
steps.content:
For periodic exponential decay, use the form $M = M_0 \cdot b^{\frac{t}{\text{period}}}$, where $M_0$ is the initial mass, $b$ is the decay factor per period, and the exponent counts how many periods have elapsed.

Substituting $M_0 = 100$, $b = 0.73$, and period $= 14.07$ minutes:
$$M = 100(0.73)^{\frac{t}{14.07}}$$That matches choice C — C is correct.
